name: prompt-engineering
description: Craft, test, and iterate on prompts for a specific LLM task. Applies prompt engineering best practices and produces optimized prompts.

task.description: The task the prompt should accomplish

input_example.description: Example input the prompt will receive

constraints.description: Token budget, response format requirements, safety constraints

steps.template:
Step 2 — Generate 3 prompt variants:

Variant A — Direct instruction (minimal tokens):
[concise, imperative style]

Variant B — Structured with examples (higher quality):
[includes role, context, examples, format spec]

Variant C — Chain-of-thought (complex reasoning):
[step-by-step reasoning instructions]

For each variant note:
- Expected token usage
- Best for: [what scenarios]
- Tradeoff: [what you sacrifice]

Step 3 — Optimization:
- Test mentally against edge cases from {{input_example}}
- Identify failure modes (ambiguity, instruction-following issues)
- Apply model-specific tricks for {{target_model}}
- Add guardrails for:
  - Hallucination prevention
  - Format compliance
  - Edge case handling
  - Graceful failure

Final output: Best prompt variant with iteration notes.
